prompt: My biggest pain with Claude on TypeScript projects is that it defaults to like 2020-era code — enums everywhere, `any` casts, namespace, class-heavy. I want a reference that anchors it to modern idiomatic TypeScript: satisfies, discriminated unions, branded types, biome over eslint+prettier, that kind of thing. Build me something so every TS session leans on it.

assertions.text: Pack directory exists at docs/references/modern-idiomatic-typescript/ (or similar slug)
INDEX.md exists with frontmatter declaring mode: idioms
INDEX.md 'Version-specific warnings' section lists at least 3 training-data defaults that the pack overrides (enum, namespace, any, etc.)
Topic files include 'Prefer X over Y' substitutions with side-by-side do/don't code examples
Topic files cover version-specific features (satisfies, const type parameters, etc.)
Topic files explicitly cover anti-patterns (enum, namespace, any casts)
Every prescription in topic files cites a source URL (Matt Pocock, TkDodo, matklad, Effective TypeScript, biome docs, etc.)
Pack references multiple distinct authoritative voices across topics — not resting on a single blog source
No raw/ directory — idioms mode is pure synthesis
CLAUDE.md has a 'modern idiomatic TypeScript' bullet with consult-when phrased around writing or refactoring TypeScript
